name: Authentication And Session Management
description: Rules for sign-in, authenticated shell entry, session continuity, and account actions.
mandatoryScenarios: Login renders both credential fields plus standard sign-in and SSO entry points.
Primary sign-in stays disabled until required credentials are present.
Valid sign-in reaches the authenticated shell cleanly.
Avatar menu exposes account actions such as Change Password and Logout.
negativeHeuristics: Incomplete credentials must not submit.
Invalid credentials must not silently navigate into the shell.
Repeated sign-in attempts must not leave duplicate loading states.
edgeHeuristics: Browser autofill should not break button enablement.
Long email values should remain readable.
securityHeuristics: Passwords must stay masked.
Protected routes must require an active session.
Logout must remove access to previously protected pages.
performanceHeuristics: Successful sign-in should reach a usable shell quickly.
Session restoration on refresh should not leave a partial shell.
accessibilityHeuristics: Visible labels should stay associated with auth controls.
Keyboard users should be able to complete and submit the form.

name: Shell Navigation And Account Access
description: Rules for the persistent shell, route switching, unread indicators, and account-menu reliability.
mandatoryScenarios: Every major post-login module linked from the rail is reachable.
Active highlighting follows the current route.
Deep-link refresh preserves the shell and correct active state.
Unread notification indicators stay aligned with the notification entry.
negativeHeuristics: Broken route transitions must not strand the user in an empty shell.
Rail highlighting must not point to the wrong module.
Unread badges must not overflow or disappear unpredictably.
edgeHeuristics: Long labels and large unread counts should remain readable.
Repeated fast navigation should not corrupt active state.
securityHeuristics: Account actions must operate only in the current authenticated session.
Shell navigation should not reveal inaccessible content before load completes.
performanceHeuristics: Route switches should preserve shell responsiveness.
Unread indicators should update without blocking navigation.
accessibilityHeuristics: Navigation labels should remain visible alongside icons.
Current section cues should not rely only on color.

name: Shared Filtering And Scope Control
description: Rules for the page-level unit multiselect and date-range controls that drive dashboard and downstream data refresh.
mandatoryScenarios: The unit selector shows the expected summary label and exposes Select All plus all available units.
All units start selected by default unless the product explicitly restores a persisted scope.
Unchecking Select All or the final remaining unit must never leave zero units selected.
Individual unit changes must keep Select All synchronized and refresh dependent widgets consistently.
The page-level date picker must support single-day and range selection and reopen with the current applied range highlighted.
Changing units must preserve the current date range, and changing dates must preserve the current unit selection.
negativeHeuristics: Rapid unit and date changes must not leave stale data.
Invalid end-before-start ranges must be blocked or normalized cleanly.
Unsupported free-text typing should not bypass picker rules.
Future dates must stay blocked when the product is intended to report only up to the current day.
edgeHeuristics: Long unit names and large unit lists should remain usable.
Leap-year, far-past, cross-month, and cross-year ranges should behave correctly.
Configured default-range behavior should stay stable across refreshes and releases.
securityHeuristics: Filters must not surface data outside the selected units and dates.
Scope changes must not briefly expose stale data from a previous filter state.
performanceHeuristics: Filter-driven reloads should show a loading state where appropriate.
Large unit lists and repeated scope updates should remain responsive.
accessibilityHeuristics: The unit selector, checkboxes, and date picker should expose visible labels and predictable focus movement.
Selected and disabled states should be perceivable without relying only on color.

name: Dashboard KPI Cards And Segregation Drilldowns
description: Rules for Home-page summary cards such as Total Observations, Risk Distribution, and configured observation-classification cards.
mandatoryScenarios: Total-style cards display a numeric total plus subordinate splits, and the split values add back to the total.
Risk-distribution cards display configured risk buckets, bucket counts, and a visual split whose proportions match the counts.
Configured observation-classification cards keep grouping mutually exclusive so a record is not counted in multiple buckets.
Card values update when units or date range change and do not retain stale counts after refresh or supported mutations.
Clicking a supported card drilldown opens exactly one contextual view and closing it retains the active page filters.
negativeHeuristics: No-data states must render intentionally instead of breaking the layout.
Tooltip percentages must not divide by zero or show misleading values.
Repeated quick clicks must not open stacked overlays.
edgeHeuristics: Extremely skewed distributions and very small bucket counts must still render correctly.
Very large counts should remain legible.
If product configuration changes classification labels, count integrity must still hold.
securityHeuristics: Card drilldowns must remain scoped to the selected units and dates.
Summary cards must not leak hidden record state through mismatched totals.
performanceHeuristics: KPI cards should refresh quickly enough to stay useful as landing-page widgets.
Tooltip and drilldown interactions should stay responsive after scope changes.
accessibilityHeuristics: Counts, legends, and status labels should remain understandable without relying only on chart color.
Drilldown triggers should have clear labels or discernible names.

name: Dashboard Trend Visualization Tabs
description: Rules for the Home-page trend area where Safety Trend and Category Trend share the same panel, scope filters, and no-data behavior.
mandatoryScenarios: The dashboard trend area exposes both Safety Trend and Category Trend and keeps units and page-level dates intact while switching.
Safety Trend plots counts across chronological dates and Category Trend plots counts against configured categories.
Tooltips show the correct date or category label plus the correct count for the hovered point or bar.
Axis labels, icons, and grid alignment stay aligned to the displayed data set.
Changing page-level units or dates refreshes the active chart view and clears stale values.
negativeHeuristics: Hovering between points or bars must not show misleading tooltip values.
No-data situations must render a clear empty state instead of a broken chart frame.
Rapid tab or filter changes must not leave the chart half rendered.
edgeHeuristics: Single-day selections, zero-count days, and long date ranges should render correctly.
Very high category counts or many categories should not distort bar alignment or icon placement.
securityHeuristics: Trend data must remain restricted to the active selected units and dates.
Chart tooltips and labels should not expose stale values from a previous filter state.
performanceHeuristics: Trend charts should remain responsive during tab switches and scope updates.
Longer date ranges should not cause visible chart corruption or browser instability.
accessibilityHeuristics: The active chart mode should be clear without depending only on color or hover.
Charts should retain visible titles and labels that communicate the current data context.

name: Life Saving Rule Category Monitoring
description: Rules for the Life Saving Rule Category section, including compact and expanded states, category segregation, Selected Dates vs Custom Dates, and section-level custom-date independence.
mandatoryScenarios: The section renders configured category cards and preserves correct totals for the active unit scope and active date mode.
Compact and Expanded presentation modes switch without overlapping or corrupting the layout.
Expanding a category reveals configured subordinate segregation, and subordinate counts add back to the category total.
Selected Dates follows the page-level date range, while Custom Dates applies its own section-level range without resetting units.
Switching back from Custom Dates restores page-level date behavior, and reopening the custom-date picker preserves the previously applied range where the product expects persistence.
negativeHeuristics: Invalid custom date ranges must not be accepted.
Changing page-level dates while Custom Dates is active must not silently overwrite the section-level custom range.
API or data-load failures in this section must degrade gracefully without breaking the rest of the dashboard.
edgeHeuristics: Long category names and large counts should remain readable.
Zero-data categories should show either zero segregation or a clear no-data state.
Rapid scope changes while a category is expanded must not leave stale segregation behind.
securityHeuristics: Category totals and custom-date views must remain limited to the selected units and correct active date context.
Section-level date overrides must not expose records outside the intended temporal scope.
performanceHeuristics: Expanding categories and switching date modes should remain responsive.
Section-level custom date changes should not stall unrelated dashboard widgets.
accessibilityHeuristics: Category headings, mode toggles, and date controls should remain clearly labeled.
Expanded and collapsed states should be perceivable without relying only on icon changes.

name: High Risk Observation Carousel And Handoff
description: Rules for the Home-page recent SIF and high-risk observation carousel, including qualification logic, auto-scroll, manual navigation, and handoff into Observations.
appliesToFeatureTypes: Safety Portal High Risk Observation Handoff
mandatoryScenarios: The carousel shows the latest qualifying observations based on the product rule for SIF and high-risk content, including correct status and risk labels.
If a configured fallback rule applies when no qualifying items exist, the header and content switch cleanly to that fallback behavior.
Automatic scrolling moves smoothly, wraps correctly, and does not break manual Next and Previous controls.
Observation thumbnails or fallback media states load without collapsing the card layout.
Clicking a carousel card navigates to the corresponding observation detail context and preserves intended filter handoff behavior.
negativeHeuristics: Closed or non-qualifying observations must not appear when the rule excludes them.
Manual navigation must not disable or corrupt automatic scrolling.
Repeated clicks must not open the wrong observation or multiple competing navigation states.
edgeHeuristics: When fewer than the expected number of qualifying records exist, the carousel should still render a stable layout without empty placeholders.
When more than the limit exist, only the newest qualifying records should be shown.
securityHeuristics: Navigation from the carousel should only open records the current user is allowed to access.
Record handoff should not reveal unrelated observation context after navigation.
performanceHeuristics: Auto-scroll and manual navigation should remain smooth and should not cause flicker or overlapping images.
Carousel refreshes should stay responsive even when qualification changes after dashboard updates.
accessibilityHeuristics: Previous and Next controls should have clear labels or discernible names.
Risk labels and record metadata should not depend only on color or imagery.

name: Dashboard Insights Assistant Interaction
description: Rules for the EOS insights drawer on the dashboard, including insight cards, suggested questions, history state, and ask flow gating.
mandatoryScenarios: The assistant area renders chat history state, insight cards, suggested questions, and the conversation composer.
The send action stays disabled until the user enters a valid prompt.
Ask EOS actions on insight cards stay aligned to the visible insight context.
The history panel reflects the current user context and updates cleanly after successful conversations.
negativeHeuristics: Empty-history states must not appear as loading failures.
Backend or inference failures must not leave the drawer unusable.
Repeated prompt submissions must not create duplicated pending states.
edgeHeuristics: Long insight narratives and longer chat histories should remain scrollable and readable.
Switching dashboard scope should not leave stale assistant content attached to the wrong filter context.
securityHeuristics: Assistant history and prompts must remain tied to the active authenticated user context.
Insight cards must not leak stale data from previous unit or date selections.
performanceHeuristics: The drawer should remain responsive while insights and conversations load.
Assistant interactions must not block surrounding dashboard controls.
accessibilityHeuristics: Insight headings, buttons, and the conversation input should remain clearly labeled.
History and suggestion regions should be keyboard reachable in a sensible order.

name: Analytics Visualization And Hierarchical Data
description: Rules for analytics pages that mix summary cards, chart panels, heat maps, and expandable treegrid data.
mandatoryScenarios: Analytics summary cards reflect the currently selected units and date range.
Visualization controls switch the visible chart mode or slice as indicated by the UI.
Narrative or summary text shown with charts stays aligned with the visible data state.
Treegrid rows expand and collapse reliably and preserve row-to-child alignment.
Heat-map or trend panels degrade gracefully when no data is returned.
negativeHeuristics: A chart-rendering failure must not break the rest of the analytics page.
Empty child datasets must not break row expansion or leave orphaned toggles.
Dense labels or legends must not overlap to the point of unusability.
edgeHeuristics: Large time-series ranges should remain readable and performant.
Large unit hierarchies should remain scrollable and expandable.
securityHeuristics: Analytics drilldowns must remain scoped to the current selected units and dates.
Inline actions inside analytics tables must not surface unrelated data.
performanceHeuristics: Heavy visualization panels should render quickly enough for normal analytics use.
Treegrid expansion and collapse should remain responsive.
accessibilityHeuristics: Charts should have surrounding titles and explanatory text.
Treegrid structure should remain keyboard-discernible where supported.

name: Timeline Review And Media Inspection
description: Rules for the observation-review workspace that combines timeline selection, sort order, scoped filtering, and selected-record detail inspection.
mandatoryScenarios: Selecting a date or timeline entry updates the right-side detail context to the matching record set.
Sort-order changes alter the visible day sequence without corrupting counts or selection context.
Time filters or category chips scope the detail pane and visible observation subset consistently.
The selected-record detail panel displays metadata, comments area, and tabs that remain tied to the current selection.
Reset actions return the timeline to a stable baseline state.
negativeHeuristics: Empty selected-day states must be clear instead of appearing broken.
Selected timeline entry and detail-pane metadata must not drift apart.
edgeHeuristics: Dense days with many observations should remain navigable.
Large date ranges should preserve scrollability and usable sort/filter feedback.
securityHeuristics: Review workspaces must not leak records outside the active scope.
Read-only inspection paths must not permit mutation without an explicit supported action.
performanceHeuristics: Day selection and detail-pane refresh should remain responsive.
Timeline reset and filter operations should not leave an inconsistent loading state.
accessibilityHeuristics: Visible headings, counters, and filters should remain clear between the two panes.
Selected-state cues should not rely solely on color.

name: Record List Selection And Master Detail
description: Cross-cutting rules for list-driven pages that combine toolbars, selectable rows or cards, detail panes, and pagination.
appliesToFeatureTypes: Safety Portal High Risk Observation Handoff
mandatoryScenarios: Lists render records with stable summary metadata and visible status cues.
Selecting a row or card updates the correct detail pane without cross-record leakage.
Bulk-action enablement follows the current selection state and disabled rows remain non-selectable.
Pagination updates the visible record slice and keeps result summaries aligned with the current page.
Sort and view-toggle controls update the list without corrupting detail selection.
negativeHeuristics: Disabled selection controls must not toggle through keyboard or pointer interaction.
Pagination or filter changes must not retain an invalid selection against the wrong records.
edgeHeuristics: Very long titles should wrap without overlapping action controls.
Large result sets should remain scrollable and pageable.
securityHeuristics: Bulk and row-level actions should remain gated by visible selection and session state.
Detail panes should never show data for a record that is no longer selected.
performanceHeuristics: List paging, sorting, and selection should remain responsive across larger result sets.
Detail-pane refresh should not stall the list surface.
accessibilityHeuristics: Nested interactive regions in list cards should preserve a sensible focus order.
Status, selected state, and disabled state should be perceivable without relying only on color.

name: Action Workflow And Audit History
description: Rules focused on action-detail metadata, workflow governance, linked observations, comments, evidence constraints, and chronological history views.
mandatoryScenarios: Action detail panels preserve alignment among status, assignee, due-date, and description metadata.
History drawers open against the currently selected action and show a chronologically sensible event stream.
Linked observations remain associated with the correct action context.
Visible comment-length and evidence-attachment constraints remain discoverable in the UI.
negativeHeuristics: History overlays must not duplicate or open against the wrong action after selection changes.
Disabled workflow actions must not become executable through inconsistent state changes.
edgeHeuristics: Long action descriptions and long history lists should remain readable and scrollable.
Large linked-observation sections should not destabilize the detail layout.
securityHeuristics: Audit history must remain scoped to the selected action and not mix entries across records.
Evidence and comment affordances should enforce visible size and type constraints.
performanceHeuristics: Opening action history should remain responsive and not block the main detail pane.
Large audit streams should remain usable without freezing surrounding controls.
accessibilityHeuristics: History and detail sections should remain clearly titled.
Drawer dismissal and navigation should remain keyboard-operable.

name: Reports And Document Access
description: Rules for report listing, report request entry, viewer resilience, and document-oriented workflows where the UI already exposes reporting surfaces.
mandatoryScenarios: Custom report entry opens a modal with the required date fields visible.
Generate Report remains gated by the required date inputs.
Cancelling the report modal closes it without mutating surrounding page state.
Report lists preserve visible metadata and page navigation behavior.
Report-related UI surfaces degrade gracefully if the embedded document viewer or worker fails.
negativeHeuristics: Generate must not proceed with missing required dates.
Viewer failures must not break the entire Reports page.
edgeHeuristics: Wide date ranges should remain valid if supported.
Large report lists and long report names should remain usable.
securityHeuristics: Report requests should remain scoped to the selected filters and optional user criteria shown in the UI.
Document-viewing surfaces should not expose unrelated content after a failed load.
performanceHeuristics: Opening the report modal and navigating the report list should remain responsive.
Document-loading failures should not stall the rest of the page.
accessibilityHeuristics: Required-field messaging in the modal should remain visible and understandable.
Modal focus handling should keep users within the report-request context until dismissed.

name: Notification Feed And Alert Triage
description: Rules for notification counts, feed browsing, unread-state integrity, per-item actions, mute settings, and safe handling of global feed actions.
mandatoryScenarios: Notification center opens with summary counts and both Observation and Action tabs visible.
Switching tabs updates the visible feed and preserves the correct count context.
Unread indicators remain aligned between the notification center and shell badge surfaces.
Feed filters and mute settings apply consistently to the current notification view.
Per-item actions target the intended notification row without affecting adjacent items.
negativeHeuristics: Global actions such as Mark All Read or Clear All must not trigger accidentally.
Broken deep links from a notification item must fail gracefully.
edgeHeuristics: High unread counts and long notification text should remain readable.
Large feeds should remain pageable without badge-count drift.
securityHeuristics: Notification content should only render for authenticated users in the current session.
Unread and clear actions should not affect the wrong item or the wrong feed type.
performanceHeuristics: Feed paging and tab switching should remain responsive under larger notification volumes.
Unread counts should refresh without leaving the shell and feed in conflicting states.
accessibilityHeuristics: Tab labels, summary headings, and notification text should remain visible and readable.
Icon-only actions should expose clear discernible names.

name: Help And Reference Content Delivery
description: Rules for tabbed reference content, category guidance, mapping guidance, and embedded user-manual delivery within the help workspace.
mandatoryScenarios: All visible help tabs render and can be switched without corrupting the page layout.
Categorization and category-mapping content remains aligned with the currently selected tab.
The User Manual tab attempts to load its document frame and shows a stable container even when the asset is unavailable.
negativeHeuristics: Missing or broken manual assets must fail gracefully instead of leaving a silent blank surface.
Rapid tab switching must not leave stale content from the previous help section behind.
edgeHeuristics: Long category and mapping lists should remain scannable.
Mixed static cards and document-frame content should not destabilize the tab layout.
securityHeuristics: Embedded reference content should remain within the expected help context.
Broken asset routing should not expose sensitive diagnostic detail to end users.
performanceHeuristics: Help tabs should switch quickly enough to support reference lookup.
A failed document load should not freeze the rest of the Help page.
accessibilityHeuristics: Tabbed help content should preserve clear labels for each tab.
Embedded manual content should expose a meaningful title or fallback text if the document cannot load.
